title: hyperfleet-api Helm Values
description: JSON Schema for the hyperfleet-api Helm chart values.yaml

replicaCount.description: Number of API server replicas to deploy

tag.description: Image tag to deploy; required, no default

imagePullSecrets.description: List of Kubernetes image pull secrets

name.description: Name of the image pull secret

nameOverride.description: Override the chart name used in resource names

fullnameOverride.description: Override the full release name used in resource names

ports.description: Container port mappings; must match the corresponding config.*.port values

api.description: API server port; must match config.server.port

health.description: Health check port; must match config.health.port

metrics.description: Metrics endpoint port; must match config.metrics.port

config.description: Application configuration; generates the ConfigMap consumed by the API server

existingConfigMap.description: Name of an existing ConfigMap to use instead of generating one; when set, all other config.* values are ignored

hostname.description: External hostname advertised by the server

host.description: Address the server binds to (use 0.0.0.0 inside Kubernetes)

port.description: Port the API server listens on

read.description: Maximum duration for reading the entire request (Go duration, e.g. 5s)

write.description: Maximum duration before timing out writes of the response (Go duration, e.g. 30s)

tls.description: TLS termination at the server level

enabled.description: Enable TLS on the API server

cert_file.description: Path to the TLS certificate file

key_file.description: Path to the TLS private key file

identity_claim.description: JWT claim to extract the caller identity from (e.g. email, sub)

identity_header.description: HTTP header carrying a pre-authenticated identity (bypasses JWT when set)

jwk.description: JWK key material for token verification

cert_file.description: Path to a local JWK certificate file

cert_url.description: URL to fetch JWK certificates from

database.description: Database connection and pool settings (credentials come from Secrets, not here)

dialect.description: Database dialect (only postgres is currently supported)

host.description: Database host; auto-set when using the built-in PostgreSQL pod

root_cert_file.description: Path to the CA certificate for SSL verification

max_connections.description: Maximum number of open connections in the pool

max_idle_connections.description: Maximum number of idle connections retained

conn_max_lifetime.description: Maximum lifetime of a connection (Go duration, e.g. 5m)

conn_max_idle_time.description: Maximum idle time before a connection is closed (Go duration, e.g. 1m)

conn_retry_attempts.description: Number of connection retry attempts on startup

conn_retry_interval.description: Delay between connection retry attempts (Go duration, e.g. 3s)

enabled.description: Enable OpenTelemetry trace context in log entries

masking.description: Sensitive data masking in log output

enabled.description: Enable automatic masking of sensitive values in logs

headers.description: HTTP header names whose values are masked in logs

fields.description: Structured log field names whose values are masked

host.description: Address the metrics server binds to (use 0.0.0.0 in Kubernetes)

port.description: Port the metrics server listens on

enabled.description: Enable TLS on the metrics endpoint

label_metrics_inclusion_duration.description: Duration window for including label-based metrics (Go duration, e.g. 168h)

reconciliation_stuck_threshold.description: Duration after which a pending reconciliation is considered stuck (Go duration, e.g. 10m)

health.description: Health and readiness probe endpoint settings

host.description: Address the health server binds to (use 0.0.0.0 in Kubernetes)

port.description: Port the health server listens on

enabled.description: Enable TLS on the health endpoint

shutdown_timeout.description: Graceful shutdown timeout before forceful termination (Go duration, e.g. 20s)

db_ping_timeout.description: Timeout for database health check pings (Go duration, e.g. 2s)

required.description: Lists of adapters that must report ready before a resource is considered ready

cluster.description: Adapters required for cluster resources (e.g. validation, dns, pullsecret, hypershift)

nodepool.description: Adapters required for nodepool resources (e.g. validation, hypershift)

create.description: Create a dedicated ServiceAccount for the deployment

annotations.description: Annotations added to the ServiceAccount (e.g. for Workload Identity)

name.description: Explicit ServiceAccount name; when empty, a name is generated from the release

podAnnotations.description: Additional annotations applied to every pod

podLabels.description: Additional labels applied to every pod

podSecurityContext.description: Pod-level security context (applies to all containers in the pod)

fsGroup.description: Filesystem group ID applied to mounted volumes

runAsNonRoot.description: Require the pod to run as a non-root user

runAsUser.description: UID the pod processes run as

securityContext.description: Container-level security context for the main API container

allowPrivilegeEscalation.description: Prevent child processes from gaining more privileges than the parent

capabilities.description: Linux capabilities to add or drop

readOnlyRootFilesystem.description: Mount the container root filesystem as read-only

resources.description: CPU and memory resource requests and limits for the API container

nodeSelector.description: Node label selectors for pod scheduling

tolerations.description: Tolerations for pod scheduling onto tainted nodes

affinity.description: Affinity and anti-affinity rules for pod scheduling

minReplicas.description: Minimum number of replicas the autoscaler will maintain

maxReplicas.description: Maximum number of replicas the autoscaler can scale to

targetCPUUtilizationPercentage.description: Target average CPU utilization percentage across all pods

targetMemoryUtilizationPercentage.description: Target average memory utilization percentage across all pods

podDisruptionBudget.description: PodDisruptionBudget configuration for voluntary disruption protection

oneOf.description: Absolute number of pods that must remain available
Percentage of pods that must remain available (e.g. 50%)

minAvailable.description: Minimum number or percentage of pods that must remain available during disruption

oneOf.description: Absolute number of pods that can be unavailable
Percentage of pods that can be unavailable (e.g. 25%)

maxUnavailable.description: Maximum number or percentage of pods that can be unavailable during disruption

database.description: Database infrastructure: choose external (production) or built-in PostgreSQL (development)

enabled.description: Use an external database instead of the built-in PostgreSQL pod

secretName.description: Name of an existing Secret containing db.host, db.port, db.name, db.user, db.password keys

postgresql.description: Built-in PostgreSQL pod for development and testing

enabled.description: Deploy a single-replica PostgreSQL pod alongside the API

port.description: Port the PostgreSQL pod listens on

resources.description: Resource requests and limits for the PostgreSQL pod

storageClass.description: StorageClass name; empty string uses the cluster default

prometheusRule.description: PrometheusRule resource for Prometheus Operator alerting

additionalLabels.description: Extra labels applied to the PrometheusRule resource

namespace.description: Namespace for the PrometheusRule; defaults to the release namespace

deletionStuck.description: Alert when a resource deletion is stuck beyond threshold

for.description: Duration the condition must hold before firing (e.g. 5m)

runbookUrl.description: URL to the runbook for this alert

deletionTimeout.description: Alert when a resource deletion exceeds the timeout window

for.description: Duration the condition must hold before firing (e.g. 30m)

runbookUrl.description: URL to the runbook for this alert

serviceMonitor.description: ServiceMonitor resource for Prometheus Operator scrape configuration

labels.description: Extra labels applied to the ServiceMonitor for Prometheus selector matching

namespace.description: Namespace for the ServiceMonitor; defaults to the release namespace

otlpEndpoint.description: OTLP exporter endpoint; traces go to stdout if empty

samplerArg.description: Sampler argument; for traceidratio this is the sampling rate (0.0-1.0)

propagators.description: Comma-separated list of context propagators (e.g. tracecontext,baggage)

nativeSidecars.description: Native sidecar containers (Kubernetes 1.28+ init containers with restartPolicy: Always); start before other init containers and run for the pod lifetime

items.description: A full Kubernetes container spec; must include restartPolicy: Always

sidecars.description: Regular sidecar containers injected into the pod; start after init containers complete

validationSchema.description: OpenAPI validation schema for cluster and nodepool specs

enabled.description: Enable spec validation; the API validates create/update requests against the schema

existingConfigMap.description: Name of an existing ConfigMap with an 'openapi.yaml' key; overrides content when set

content.description: Inline OpenAPI 3.0 schema content defining ClusterSpec and NodePoolSpec

extraEnv.description: Additional environment variables injected into the API container; use sparingly, prefer config.* values

extraVolumeMounts.description: Additional volume mounts for the API container

extraVolumes.description: Additional volumes attached to the pod
